column1: # Retromod — Forge SRG → Mojang member-name mappings
# KIND is FIELD or METHOD. Comments start with '#'. Blank lines are ignored.
# Owner class is intentionally NOT recorded — SRG names are globally unique
# across the entire MC namespace, so a single SRG → Mojang mapping is
# sufficient regardless of the field/method's owner. The transformer's
# ClassRemapper applies these blindly to any matching SRG-pattern reference.
# Forge mods built with ForgeGradle's reobfJar task (essentially every Forge
# mod for MC 1.17 .. 1.20.4) reference MC members by Forge's SRG names —
# fields like Blocks.f_50069_ (= STONE) and methods like
# Component.m_237113_ (= literal). Forge's runtime classloader used to remap
# these to the actual MC names at class load time. Forge 64.x for MC 26.1
# dropped the SRG remap layer because MC 26.1 has no obfuscation at all
# (Mojang shipped official names directly). Reobf'd mods then break with
# NoSuchFieldError / NoSuchMethodError on every SRG name. Retromod takes over
# PROVENANCE — how this file is generated (and how to regenerate it)
# Composed by joining two upstream mapping sources on obfuscated names,
# within each obfuscated class — exactly the join ForgeGradle/SrgUtils
# perform to produce a Mojang-named dev environment:
#   2. Mojang official mappings (official <-> obf), MC 1.20.1 client
#   join: for each obf class, FIELDS match on obf field name (unique per
#   class); METHODS match on (obf name + obf descriptor), the descriptor
#   rebuilt from Mojang's signature via the obf class map. The descriptor
#   getPath all share obf name "a", resolved by their descriptors).
# SRG ids are version-stable, so 1.20.1 coverage applies to the whole
# are filtered — a reobf'd mod never references MC's synthetics by SRG.
# This file previously shipped a 117-entry hand-curated starter set. 56 of
# those 117 entries were WRONG (verified against the upstream sources above):
# e.g. f_50122_ was GRAVEL but is GRANITE; ResourceLocation m_135815_/m_135827_
# method — it is tryParse). All are now correct.
# MCPConfig data (c) Forge Development LLC, distributed under a zlib-style
# permissive license; this is an altered/derived work (SRG names extracted
# and re-keyed to Mojang names). Mojang official names are used under
# Mojang's mapping terms for development/interoperability purposes — this
# file is a transformed derivative (no obfuscation, no descriptors, no class
# structure), NOT a complete unmodified copy of the official mappings.
# A given SRG name maps to exactly one Mojang name; the loader's HashMap
# keeps the last of any duplicate KIND+SRG. The generator emits each SRG
